description: Explore how current fabric init execution can be refactored into a plan-driven install state machine with preflight, plan, confirm, execute, and post-setup phases.
focus: init.ts orchestration; stage extraction; install plan abstraction; result model
findings: `packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts` already has an implicit pipeline: preflight (`resolveDevMode`, `detectClientSupports`, TTY-only `printInitPlanSummary`) -> scaffold (`initFabric`) -> stage execution (`bootstrap`/`mcp`/`hooks`) -> summary/reason output. Refactor seam: extract `buildInitPlan()` before any writes, with steps for scaffold, bootstrap, mcp, hooks, and a typed result model richer than current `InitStageRecord` (`ran|skipped|failed`). Reusable executors already exist in `bootstrap.ts`, `config.ts`, `hooks.ts`; wrap them as plan actions. Main risk: current `initFabric()` performs eager writes and aborts on existing paths, so preflight/confirm must happen before it. Preserve TTY/non-TTY behavior and i18n/doc contract in locales/docs.

description: Explore prompt and terminal UI implementation options for rewriting fabric init as a guided installer, with emphasis on inquirer vs @clack/prompts and TTY/non-TTY behavior.
focus: prompt library; citty integration; spinner/summary UI; non-interactive fallback
findings: `packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts` has no prompt lib today; `citty` only parses args/dispatches (`src/index.ts`), while init UX is plain `console.log`/`stderr` plus a hard TTY gate via `isInteractiveInit()` and color gating in `src/colors.ts`. Existing reusable UI pieces: plan summary, stage summary, capability table, localized strings in `packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/{en,zh-CN}.ts`. Non-TTY is an explicit contract in docs/tests: `docs/initialization.md` says no interactive takeover in CI, and `__tests__/init-mcp-scope.test.ts` + `helpers/init-test-utils.ts` assert behavior by mocking `process.stdout/stderr.isTTY`. For a guided installer, `@clack/prompts` fits better than Inquirer: ESM-friendly, lighter, built-in spinner/intro/outro; but keep argv flags as source of truth and bypass prompts entirely when !TTY/CI to avoid breaking snapshots and automation.

description: Explore the new CLI surface for a breaking init redesign, including flags, command semantics, migration of old options, and advanced subcommand positioning.
focus: init command UX; --yes; --plan; --reapply; old flag mapping; command help
findings: Current init surface is one-shot: target/debug/--force, negative --no-bootstrap/--no-mcp/--no-hooks via boolean args, --interactive, and --mcp-install global|local in packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts. No --yes, --plan, or --reapply exists yet; “plan” is only an interactive TTY summary printed before writes, not dry-run. Reapply semantics currently map closest to destructive --force, while targeted reruns are advanced commands: bootstrap/config/hooks install; hooks is hidden at top-level but exposed under config. Breaking redesign must migrate old skip flags, clarify --force vs --reapply, add help/i18n strings, and update docs still describing one-shot init plus old skips.

description: Explore the documentation and test blast radius required by a breaking init redesign, including snapshots, onboarding docs, and acceptance coverage.
focus: README; docs/getting-started; docs/initialization; init tests; snapshots
findings: Blast radius is broad: public onboarding is duplicated across README.md, docs/getting-started.md, and docs/initialization.md, all describing the current 7-stage `fabric init` flow, exact artifacts (`.fabric/bootstrap/README.md`, `.agents/skills/fabric-init/SKILL.md`, `.codex/hooks.json`), and follow-up prompts. Tests hard-code this contract in packages/cli/__tests__/init-*.test.ts plus locale/snapshot coverage in packages/cli/__tests__/i18n.test.ts and __snapshots__/i18n.test.ts.snap. A breaking redesign must update both locale files (`packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts`, `zh-CN.ts`) and acceptance assertions for stage names, reason/capability text, hook behavior, non-destructive/force semantics, and `--mcp-install` global/local paths. Reusable test base: packages/cli/__tests__/helpers/init-test-utils.ts. Main migration risk is doc/test drift from duplicated stage narrative.
